title: Money & percentages
subtitle: Practical money maths for offers, savings, tax, travel and budgets.
curriculumLinks: Module 1: budgeting and value for money
examContexts: saving a fixed amount per week

rules.title: Find a percentage of an amount
rules.explanation: Change the percentage to a decimal, then multiply by the full amount.
steps: Add units such as EUR, kg or cm if needed.

rules.title: Discount means take away
rules.explanation: A discount lowers the price. Find the discount amount first, then subtract it from the original price.
steps: Subtract the discount from the original price.
Check the sale price is smaller than the original price.

examples.question: A hoodie costs EUR 60 and has 25% off.

rules.title: Weekly saving is division
rules.explanation: If the same amount is saved each week, divide the target by the weekly saving. If there is a remainder, one extra week is needed.
rules.formula: weeks = target / amount saved each week
steps: Divide the full cost by the weekly saving.
If the answer is not a whole number, round up to the next week.
Check by multiplying weeks by weekly saving.

examples.question: A game costs EUR 92. You save EUR 10 each week.

rules.title: Exchange rates multiply or divide
rules.explanation: Read the rate carefully. If EUR 1 equals dollars, multiply euro by the rate to change euro into dollars.
steps: Write down what EUR 1 is worth.
Multiply if changing euro into the other currency.
Divide if changing the other currency back into euro.
